Bafilomycin F treatment blocks the protective effect of preconditioning on postischemic left ventricular diastolic function and infarct size in a perfused heart model.

Phenylaminoadenosine, an A2a adenosine receptor analog, does not affect the time to ischemic contracture in models of myocardial stunning.

Among ureides with five acyl carbons, 1-(2-chloro-3-methylbutanoyl)urea is almost twice as potent as its 2-methyl analogue.

Valine methyl ester, a nonpeptidic compound, does not inhibit [14C]glycylsarcosine uptake by the basolateral peptide transporter in human intestinal Caco-2 cells.

L-valine methyl ester has a higher affinity for the peptide transporter PEPT2 than for PEPT1.
3-(2-bromopropionylamino)-benzoylurea (JIMB01) demonstrates promising in vivo efficacy in a hepatocarcinoma (Bel-7402) tumor model.
d-leucine hydroxamic acid inhibits Aeromonas proteolytica aminopeptidase with a K i of 2.0 nM, which is much stronger than its l-leucine hydroxamic acid analog.
D-leucine hydroxamic acid exhibits a Ki value of 4.3 × 10⁻⁶ M for its enzyme target, indicating significant inhibitory activity despite the absence of the α-amino group.

(S)-2-guanidino-3-methylbutyric acid is inactive as an antagonist at the GABA receptor in the assay conditions described.

3-(2-bromopropionylamino)-benzoylurea (JIMB01) is a small molecule compound with strong antiproliferative activity against thirteen human tumor cell lines, including multidrug-resistant B-cell lymphoma.

i-Valeramide exhibits permeation rates into both living and proto-Tetraethyleneglycol dimethylether cell types, with permeation dominated by hydrogen bond acidity and smaller contributions from polarity/polarizability and hydrogen bond basicity.

cis,cis-muconate induces the expression of both protocatechuate 3,4-dioxygenase and catechol 1,2-dioxygenase in strain 1CP cells.

cis,cis-muconic acid is used as a substrate to measure PAA (peroxidase) activity, with a molar absorption coefficient of 16,000 M⁻¹ protein A1 cm⁻¹.
The fumarate nitrate reduction pathway in Salmonella typhimurium regulates fis, a virulence-related protein.
The mechanism of WhiB3 4Fe-4S cluster degradation is similar to that of the fumarate nitrate regulator.
Sorbic acid nitrite treatment may increase the mitotic index by affecting the spindle apparatus.

Trans-3-chloroacrylic acid is produced as a dead-end product during the growth of Pseudomonas sp. EK1, while R. erythropolis strains EK2 and EK5 degrade this compound.

2-fluoro-cis,cis-muconate is a main degradation product of 2-fluorophenol by phenol-grown cells of Exophiala species and accumulates in the liquid media after degradation of fluorotoluenes by fungi.
2-fluoro-cis,cis-muconate is a relatively poor substrate for the pJP4-encoded chloromuconate cycloisomerase TfdD, showing the lowest specificity constant among tested cis,cismuconates.
trans-3-chloroacrylic acid is a metabolite produced by Pseudomonas sp. EK1 and acts as a dead-end product in the DCPE degradation pathway.
n-hexylamine sorbate exhibits antifungal activity against pathogenic fungi.
cVA induces gender-specific differences in behavioral response in Drosophila, which are derived from sexually dimorphic third-order olfactory neurons.

2-fluoro-cis,cis-muconate is a substrate for chloromuconate cycloisomerase from R. eutropha JMP134, exhibiting high substrate affinity but relatively low catalytic activity (k cat).
4-carbomethoxy-2-(2'-hydroxyphenyl)benzoxazole is a simplified analog of UK-1 that displays cytotoxic activity against cancer cell lines and forms complexes with metal ions such as Mg2+ and Zn2+.
4-carbomethoxy-2-(2'-hydroxyphenyl)benzoxazole is a minimalized UK-1 analog that retains anticancer activity against cancer cell lines even when modified at the C4 position with certain groups.

In APP SW /PS1 dE9 transgenic mice, 2-[(pyridine-2-ylmethyl)-amino]-phenol ameliorates Aβ pathology and rescues memory deficits.

Curcumin induces paraptosis cell death in the A172 cell line.
Dihydroxyphenyl acetate has no effect on THir neuron survival at concentrations up to 300 µM after 24 hours in primary rat mesencephalic cultures.
Curcumin is a polyphenol with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anticancer properties.
Neural toxicity associated with methylphenyl-1,2,4,6-tetrahydropyridine is diminished by systemic administration of cystamine.

N'-(4-methylbenzylidene)-5-phenylisoxazole-3-carbohydrazide significantly prevents MPTP-induced neurotoxicity in a mouse model of Parkinson's disease, as shown by improved gait behavior in the footprint test and increased grip strength in the horizontal wire test.
Curcumin is a natural polyphenol with anti-inflammatory properties and is considered a promising candidate for natural anti-inflammatory agents without significant side effects.
The anti-inflammatory activity of curcumin involves multiple mechanisms, including inhibition of inflammatory mediators and modulation of immune cell function.
3-(4-pyridyl)acrylamide has potential neuroprotective effects and may be relevant for Parkinson's disease treatment.
4-(4-ethyl-piperazin-1-yl)-1-(2,3,4-trihydroxy-phenyl)butan-1-one (SP-04m) protects against Aα-42-induced neurotoxicity in neuronal cells in vitro.

L-764406 is a potent ligand for human PPAR␥ (hPPAR␥) and binds covalently to the PPAR␥ ligand-binding domain (LBD), as demonstrated by mass spectral analysis showing a molecular mass increase in the LBD upon incubation with L-764406.
L-764406 is a non-thiazolidinedione compound that acts as a high affinity and specific agonist for PPAR␥, binding covalently to Cys 313 in helix 3 of the ligand-binding domain of human PPAR␥.
L-764406 has a molecular structure and relatively small size that suggest it may interact covalently with PPAR␥.
Bis(phenylsulfonyl)methane undergoes intermolecular addition to dienes in the presence of [(π-allyl)PdCl]2, dppp, and NaOMe as a reducing reagent.
N-benzothiazolyl-2-benzenesulfonamide derivatives induce ABCA1/Abca1 mRNA and protein content in vitro and in vivo through activation or induction of LXR-alpha/LXRA/Lxra and/or LXR-beta/LXRB/Lxrb.

N-benzensulfonyl 2-(2-pyridylethyl) -benzimidazole derivatives have been synthesized and studied for their antiviral activity.

TNBS induces the CES1A1 promoter and is implicated in the induction of CES1A1 by antioxidants.

N-(benzothiazol-2-yl)benzenesulfonamide can serve as a lead compound for the development of more potent antitumor agents.
2,3-bis(acetyl mercaptomethyl)-quinoxalin is an inhibitor of poliovirus replication and also inhibits the multiplication of herpes simplex virus (HSV).
4-anilino-6-nitro-2-tosylmethylquinazoline derivatives exhibit antiplasmodial activity, with certain 3-substituted analogs showing IC50 values as low as 0.95 mM and 1.3 mM.

N-methylmaysenine is a maytansinoid for which the core structure and a key intermediate have been synthesized via a route involving the vinylogous Mukaiyama aldol reaction and macrolactonization.

7-(2,3-epoxypropoxy)actinomycin D retains DNA binding property and enhances in vivo tumor-inhibiting activity compared to actinomycin D analogues.
Benzodioxole isothiocyanate is used as a synthetic precursor for 1,2-disubstituted thioureas with potent anticancer activity.

3-Phenyl-4-hydroxycoumarin derivatives have been screened as inhibitors of HIV-1-protease.
A phenyloxazoline-based hydroxamate does not inhibit Aquifex aeolicus LpxC.
Integramycin is a complex natural product with significant and novel activity against HIV-1 integrase, making it a promising candidate for new anti-HIV therapy.

Pyridazino[3,4-b][1,5]benzoxazepin-5(6H)-one derivatives have been found to exhibit HIV-1 reverse transcriptase inhibition.
A phenyloxazoline-based hydroxamate inhibits Escherichia coli LpxC with a K(i) of approximately 50 nM.
Oxochromenyl xanthenone derivatives have been evaluated for their binding affinity to the non-nucleoside inhibitor binding pocket (NNIBP) of HIV-1 reverse transcriptase in molecular docking studies.

N-6 lipophilic substituents of intermediate size, such as n-propyl, i-propyl, cyclopropylmethyl, and allyl, enhance the inhibitory activity of pyridazino [3,4-b] [1,5]benzoxazepin-5(6H)-one analogs against HIV-1 reverse transcriptase.
Oximino-piperidino-piperidine compounds serve as CCR5 antagonists, targeting the HIV-1 entry receptor for potential antiretroviral drug development.
NBP is a parent compound for several derivatives with enhanced antiplatelet and antithrombotic properties, including increased antioxidant levels and improved neurobehavioral outcomes.

3-n-butyl-2,3-dihydro-1H-isoindol-1-one exhibits antiplatelet activity, likely through modulation of the AA-thromboxane A2 cascade and involvement of the NO-cyclic guanosine monophosphate signaling pathway.

NBP possesses antiplatelet aggregation and antithrombotic effects, including inhibition of platelet aggregation and serotonin release, and increases cyclic adenosine monophosphate (cAMP) levels in platelets.
3-butylbenzo[c]thiophen-1(3H)-one has reported actions against free radicals, platelet aggregation, thrombosis, and cerebral ischemia.
3-butylbenzo[c]thiophen-1(3H)-one, also known as 5d, is an analogue of 3-n-butylphthalide designed to improve therapeutic effect.
Etazepine is a new anticonvulsant that protects mice and rats from a wide variety of convulsant agents at doses 16–45 times lower than those causing neurotoxic effects.
α-aminovalerophenone derivatives directly block dopamine uptake in rat brain synaptosomes and transfected HEK293 cells.
3-aminoethyl-1-tetralone compounds have been synthesized and their binding affinities to D2, 5-HT2A, and 5-HT2C receptors have been explored.
AOAA is an anticonvulsant agent whose effects against penicillin-induced seizures diminish with pretreatment times beyond 75 minutes.
2-(8-(1-(3-chlorobenzyl)piperidin-4-ylamino)octyl)isoindoline-1,3-dione can cross the blood-brain barrier.
3-Butyl-6-methylpiperazine-2,5-dione, identified as antimicrobial compounds extracted from Bacillus subtilis (PSB5), shows activity against Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. gerberae.
6-methoxy n-butyl phthalide undergoes oxidative metabolism in rats following oral administration, producing six detectable metabolites in urine within 0–24 hours.
77Br-labelled brombenperidol shows D2-specific localization in rat brain, indicating its utility as a D2 receptor ligand in PET studies.
Acute administration of umespirone produces anxiolytic-like effects in mouse light/dark transition and rat social interaction tests.
R-(+)-3-(3-hydroxyphenyl)-N-propylpiperidine hydrochloride, a sigma-acting compound, does not alter methamphetamine-induced neurotoxicity in mice.
3-butyl-6-methylpiperazine-2,5-dione is a diketopiperazine (DKP) compound detected in dichloromethane extracts from Bacillus amyloliquefaciens (UQ154).
Etazepine exerts its anticonvulsant effects by activating the GABAergic system.
Phenylbutyrophenone derivatives function as antagonists at the 5-HT 2A, 5-HT 2C, and DA D 2 receptors, with selectivity profiles influenced by substituents at the phenyl ring.
Umespirone administered for 2 weeks does not prevent the development of withdrawal signs after benzodiazepine treatment.
Amylobarbitone is used as a reference drug for achieving similar maternal and fetal blood levels when compared to Phenobarbitone.
Copper dichromate dihydrate (CuCr 2 O 7 Ç(H 2 O) 2) is present as a dominant component in CCA-treated wood waste after PFL treatment.
Chromium(II) dichromate (HCr 2 O 7-) can exchange its anions with quaternary ammonium cations in aqueous phase to facilitate extraction of lipophilic ion pairs into the organic phase.
Silver dichromate is used to oxidize organically bound chlorine in monochloroacetic acid, facilitating its quantification in urine.
Silver chromate is relatively soluble, as no insoluble remnants were visible in light microscope after 1 hour treatment of Golgi-impregnated sections in distilled water.
Silver dichromate and silver chromate exist in equilibrium at pH 6, with the reaction Cr2O3·2Cr03·2H2O, and silver chromate is several times less soluble than silver dichromate.
Silver dichromate (Ag2Cr2O7) reagent is used to detect chlorophenyl-cysteines and -mercapturic acids on paper chromatography by producing a colored zone.
Silver chromate is the fibrillar precipitate formed in nervous tissue as a result of Golgi chromate-silver impregnation, as confirmed by X-ray diffraction.
Chromium(II) dichromate is a chromium chemical produced for use in pigments, chromate production, and other industrial applications.

Nickel chromate-manganese(II) oxide (NiCrMnO4) is an oxide phase that can form in Ni-based superalloys during oxidation, particularly in the presence of manganese and chromium, with formation observed between 900 and 1000 °C.
Quinolinium dichromate-mediated oxidation of diols proceeds almost twice as fast in D2O than in H2O.

SCC may inhibit mutagen activity by forming complexes that trap mutagens, potentially accelerating their transit through the digestive tract or interfering with metabolic activation and degradation of activated mutagen species.
SCC forms complexes with chlorophyll, which is believed to stabilize the complex and facilitate the degradation of SCC-derived mutagens, preventing their migration and DNA coupling.

A highly enantioselective mutant of cyclohexanone monooxygenase (CHMO) oxidized methyl-p-methylbenzyl thioether to (R) sulfoxide with 98.7% enantiomeric excess.
RAPTA-C is highly selective and efficient against metastatic cancer cells in vivo but is weakly cytotoxic in vitro.

Cyclohexanone monooxygenase from Acinetobacter calcoaceticus NCIMB 9871, when mutated, can convert methyl-p-methylbenzylsulfide with enantiomeric excess improved from 14%ee to 99%ee, showing both (R)- and (S)-selectivity.

Methyl-p-methylbenzyl thioether can be oxidized enantioselectively in the air to form chiral sulfoxides, with the wild-type CHMO from Acinetobacter sp. NCIMB 9871 yielding only 14% enantiomeric excess in favor of the (R)-sulfoxide.
RAPTA-C has low solubility and rapid degradation prior to reaching the target, limiting its efficacy.
RAPTA-C is a dichlororuthenium(II) complex with a unique scaffold containing a Ru(II) center, an arene ligand, a phosphine ligand, and chloride ligands.
4-isopropylbenzylamine shows high affinity for phosphatidylcholine (PCh) among the compounds tested, though less than chlorphentermine.
I4c-PAH is used as a substrate to measure PAH uptake in rabbit proximal tubule cells grown on collagen-coated filters, with assays performed at 0.1 mM I4c-PAH and 1 mM probenecid at room temperature.

Cumene hydroperoxide at low concentrations serves as a substrate for glutathione peroxidase in isolated hepatocytes, leading to marked changes in the cellular redox system and production of only small amounts of lipid peroxides.

Cumene hydroxyperoxide induces a time-dependent sequence of cellular events in PC12 cells, including decreases in cellular ATP content and mitochondrial membrane potential within 10–30 min, followed by reduced MTT reduction, decreased cellular GSH, actin filament disorganization, and detachment from the substratum between 30–60 min, and late manifestations such as intracellular calcium accumulation and plasma membrane permeabilization after 60–120 min.
Cumene hydroxyperoxide is used as a peroxide donor in reconstitution attempts to reconstitute the activity of CYP125RHA1.
4-isopropylbenzyl phenol (BHPF) is a BPA alternative that dramatically inhibits the proliferation of breast cancer cells and exhibits anti-estrogenic activity comparable to 4-hydroxytamoxifen.
DOT induces dose-dependent reductions in the weight of the thymus, spleen, and lymph node in vivo.

Cumene hydroxyperoxide is used as a substrate in the assay for glutathione peroxidase (GPX) activity, where the decrease in absorbance of NADPH at 340 nm is measured to determine enzyme activity at pH 7.6.
The ground state and several radical excited states of p-MePhS can be optimized using CASSCF/AVTZ methods, with excitation energies evaluated at the CASPT2/AVTZ level.
Bis(4-methylphenyl)acetylene reacts with (4-methylphenyl)boronic acid under Pd(OAc)2 catalysis and Ag2CO3 oxidation in n-PrOH/H2O to form the corresponding hexaarylbutadiene.
Platencin exhibits a minimum detection concentration (MDC) of 2 ng in an agar-diffusion two-plate differential sensitivity antisense assay for FabF in Staphylococcus aureus.
Platencin has a whole-cell activity IC50 of 0.19 g/ml for fatty acid synthesis inhibition in Staphylococcus aureus and 2.7 g/ml in Streptococcus pneumoniae.

Platencin inhibits phospholipid biosynthesis in whole-cell labeling experiments in Staphylococcus aureus with an IC50 of 0.19 g/ml (0.45 M) and in Streptococcus pneumoniae with an IC50 of 2.7 g/ml (6.3 M).
The minimum detection concentration (MDC) of platencin in an agar-diffusion two-plate differential sensitivity antisense assay for FabF is 2 ng in Staphylococcus aureus.

Platencin shows MIC values of 0.5 g/ml against S. aureus (MSSA), 0.5 g/ml against S. aureus plus serum, 1 g/ml against S. aureus (MRSA), 1 g/ml against S. aureus (MRSA, macrolide R), 3 g/ml against S. aureus (MRSA, linezolid R), 0.5 g/ml against S. aureus (VISA, vancomycin I), 1 g/ml against E. faecalis (macrolide R), 0.1 εg/ml against E. faecium (vancomycin R), 1 g/ml against S. pneumoniae, and 4 g/ml against E. coli (tolC).
Adamantaplatensimycin is a novel analogue of platensimycin with potent activity against drug-resistant strains such as MRSA and VREF.
Adamantaplatensimycin is a compound with two enantiomers, (À)-2 and (+)-2, that display different biological activities against MRSA and VREF.
The enantiomer (À)-2 of adamantaplatensimycin exhibits minimum inhibitory concentrations of 1.3–1.8 mg/mL against MRSA and VREF.
The enantiomer (À)-2 of adamantaplatensimycin shows no activity against MRSA and VREF at concentrations up to 88 mg/mL.
Adamantaplatensimycin is a synthetic analogue of platensimycin in which the adamantyl moiety forms a cage-like structure mimicking the ketolide domain of the parent molecule.
Ethyl-2,4-dihydroxybenzoic acid, a prolyl hydroxylase inhibitor, inhibits the generation of both pro ␣ 1(I) and pro ␣ 2(I) collagen in A10 cells after 2 days of treatment.
Tofacitinib citrate can be used as monotherapy or in combination with methotrexate or other nonbiologic DMARDs for rheumatoid arthritis treatment.
Tofacitinib citrate is a Janus kinase (JAK) inhibitor used as an antiarthritic agent effective after oral administration for the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis.
Mature ␣ 2(I) collagen is completely suppressed in its generation by 250 mM ethyl-2,4-dihydroxybenzoic acid, while mature ␣ 1(I) collagen is not completely inhibited.
Acitemate reduces vascular changes and inflammation in adjuvant arthritis in rats on high cholesterol diets.

Adenostemmoic acid B exhibits anti-inflammatory effects in murine macrophages by reducing TNF secretion and nitric oxide production, and increasing IL-10 production in response to LPS and other TLR agonists.
Tofacitinib citrate is indicated for adult patients with moderately to severely active rheumatoid arthritis who have had an inadequate response or intolerance to methotrexate.
Adenostemmoic acid B induces abnormal cytosolic membranous structures in Trypanosoma cruzi epimastigotes at a concentration of 20 µM after 48 hours of treatment.
Triptotriterpenic acid A is identified as an effective-toxic component in Tripterygium Glycosides Tablets, implicated in both anti-rheumatoid arthritis and hepatotoxicity effects.
3-Hydroxymonoethylglycinexylidide is a major unconjugated metabolite of xylidide and is quantified in plasma using liquid chromatography with a validated procedure.
The pyridyldithio moiety of N-succinimidyl-4-(2-pyridyldithio)butyrate reacts with the sulfhydryl group of DM4 to form a hindered disulfide bond in antibody-drug conjugates.

N-succinimidyl-4-(2-pyridyldithio)butyrate is an optimized cleavable linker used in antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) to attach a payload to an antibody, enabling controlled drug release inside target cells.
The succinimidyl group of N-succinimidyl-4-(2-pyridyldithio)butyrate reacts with amino groups of lysine residues on antibodies to form stable amide bonds.
N-succinimidyl-4-(2-pyridyldithio)butanoate (SPDB) is used to introduce pyridylthio groups onto antibodies for conjugation purposes.
N-succinimidyl-4-(2-pyridyldithio)butanoate (SPDB) is a commercially available pyridyldisulfide linker used for chemical conjugation.
Oral administration of CHR-2863 is efficacious against rodent malaria in vivo.
Hydroxyethylamine-phthalimide analogs can significantly decrease blood parasite load in chloroquine-resistant Plasmodium berghei and P. berghei ANKA infected mouse models.
Beta-carbolinium compounds exhibit medium suppressive activity in vivo against rodent malaria.

(S)-7-chloro-N-(4-methyl-1-(4-methylpiperazin-1-yl)pentan-2-yl)-quinolin-4-amine triphosphate demonstrates good solid-state properties and promising in vitro and in vivo activity against experimental malaria models.
Tetrahydroisoquinoline-4-carboxamide scaffolds are structurally related to PfATP4 inhibitors and are associated with activity against late-stage gametocytes in Plasmodium falciparum.
Lucanthone N-oxide exhibits greater antischistosomal activity than lucanthone in mice.

Lead acridone compounds exhibit potent in vitro and in vivo antimalarial activity against both blood and liver stages of Plasmodium, including efficacy against chloroquine-sensitive and multi-drug resistant parasites.
N1,N2-bis(7-chloroquinolin-4-yl)cyclohexane-1, 2-diamine exhibits potent antimalarial activity against Plasmodium berghei in mice and is orally active.
4'-dehydroxy-4'-fluoroamodiaquine exhibits antimalarial activity in the low nanomolar range.

SPH3127 is a compound whose human plasma clearance and volume of distribution can be predicted using simple four-species allometric scaling, and its oral human plasma concentration-time profile matches phase I clinical trial pharmacokinetic data.

Hydroxyethylamine-phthalimide analogs exhibit marked killing effects on blood stage Plasmodium and inhibit gametocyte stages (III-V) of P. falciparum in culture and liver-stage infection of P. berghei in both in vitro and in vivo models.
4-amino-7-chloroquinoline-thiazolidinone hybrids display lower antimalarial activity compared to 4-benzylamino-7-chloroquinoline derivatives.

Hydroxyethylamine-phthalimide analogs inhibit the blood stage of Plasmodium falciparum at submicromolar concentrations, with the best compounds showing IC50 values of around 500 nM against drug-resistant P. falciparum.
The in vivo potency of 4-amino-3-phenylquinolin-2(1H)-one is higher than expected, possibly due to reduced plasma protein binding compared to related analogues.
Aminohydroxyethylsulfonamide derivatives exhibit better in vitro antimalarial activity against Plasmodium falciparum compared to hydroxyethylamine derivatives.
For B n (NH 2 ) n (n = 4, 5, 6), the classical nonplanar ring structure is more stable than the nonclassical deltahedron, especially when n is small.
Planar N-thianaphthylmethyl analogues of (+)-(3R,4R)-dimethyl-4-(3-hydroxyphenyl)piperidine have significantly lower binding affinities for the opioid receptor compared to non-planar analogues.
5-Methyl N-Methylmorphinan-6-one derivatives can affect the 3–7 lock switch in the μ-opioid receptor, as studied by molecular docking and dynamics.
N-substituted rac-cis-N-methyl-1,2,3,4,4a,9a-hexahydrobenzofuro[2,3-c]pyridin-6-ol analogues have high affinity for opioid receptors and exhibit significant antinociceptive activity.
The t-Hpp1 analogue of the delta opioid receptor selective tetrapeptide displays delta receptor binding affinity similar to the parent Tyr1-containing peptide.
Fumaramate methyl ester derivatives of naltrexone and oxymorphone act as reversible agonists on the guinea pig ileal longitudinal muscle preparation.
Agonist activity of capsaicin analogues in vitro is predictive of antinociceptive activity in vivo.

The opioid activity of the diphenyl-propylamine series is strongly influenced by steric factors, specifically the presence and integrity of two phenyl rings, as described by the Snyder receptor aromatic-site theory.
Analogues of endomorphin-2 containing (2S)-2-methyl-3-(2,6-dimethyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propanoic acid as the N-terminal amino group are moderately potent mu opioid antagonists.

N-glycoconjugation of [Leu5]enkephalin with beta-D-glucopyranosylamine results in a peptide with higher potency in the guinea pig ileum assay and lower potency in the mouse vas deferens assay, indicating decreased delta opioid receptor selectivity.
The maleimidoacetamide derivative (10) exhibits irreversible agonism at opioid receptors, with greater irreversible j.L antagonism in the MVD than in the GPI.
1-n-hexyl substitution in dihydrobenzofuran-7-carboxamides confers high stereospecificity in D2 receptor affinity and inhibitory activity.

6-hydroxy-2-aminotetralin-2-carboxylic acid (Hat) can be substituted for Tyr(1) in the message domain of deltorphin II analogues to yield peptides with high delta opioid receptor selectivity and subnanomolar IC50 values as agonists in the mouse vas deferens assay.

14-hydroxymorphinan is a chemical scaffold that can be modified at ring C to yield analogues with potent binding affinity (approximately 1 nM) at both kappa and mu opioid receptors, acting as agonists at kappa and partial agonists or antagonists at mu receptors.
Naphthoylindole compounds have been evaluated for their affinities at the CB1 receptor.
Prolyl-phenylalanylamide derivatives with N-methylbenzylamide substitutions retain activity on the rabbit NK 1 receptor.
Naltrexonazine is a selective mu(1)-opioid receptor antagonist that does not reduce the antitussive effect of endomorphin-2 in mice.
Dimeric amino(methyl)thiazole derivatives exhibit increased high-affinity selectivity for the human histamine H2 receptor compared to monomeric analogues.
In 4-piperidinylbenzamides, electronic properties primarily determine biological activity, as shown by QSAR studies.

Direct hydrogen bonding interactions between the receptor and groups attached to the 4-position of 4-amino-3-phenylquinolin-2(1H)-one are crucial for high binding affinity at the NMDA receptor glycine site.
Karimunone B is a marine microbial metabolite reported from Indonesia and is classified as a fungal metabolite.

Karimunone B is an isocoumarine compound isolated from the endophytic fungus Fusarium sp. KJMT.FP.4.3 collected from the Indonesian sponge Xestospongia sp., and it exhibits antibacterial activity against multidrug resistant Salmonella enterica ser. Typhi with a minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) of 125 μg/mL.
Echisoside inhibits proliferation and induces apoptosis in AGS (adenocarcinoma gastric) cancer cells in vitro.
Rubrofusarin, a xanthone, can affect the rate of dehydrogenation of isopropyl alcohol to acetone by Fusarium lini Bolley (FlB).
Rubrofusarin is a compound isolated from the bioactive methanol-soluble extract of Cassia obliqua stem and fruits that exhibits antimycobacterial activity.
5-carbomethoxymethyl-7-hydroxy-2-pentylchromone exhibits antitumor activity against MCF7 and lung cancer cell lines.
Echisoside induces morphological changes characteristic of apoptotic cell death and causes cell cycle arrest at the G1 phase in AGS cancer cells.
Artocarpetin is a flavone analog with high inhibitory activity against mushroom tyrosinase, which is substantially decreased by methoxylation at the C7 position.
Echisoside upregulates bax and caspase3 expression and downregulates cyclin D1, bcl2, VEGFA, c-myc, and survivin in AGS cancer cells.
Rubrofusarin is a cytotoxic naphthopyrone compound isolated from the Peruvian plant Cassia quinquangulata Rich.

2,2′-dihydroxy-4′,6′-dimethoxychalcone exhibits selective cytotoxic activity against gastric carcinoma SGC-7901 cells with an IC50 of 1.6 μg/ml, which is more potent than the reference compound mitomycin C (IC50 = 13.0 μg/ml).
In P. fluorescens strain Q2-87, the products of the 2,4-DAPG biosynthetic genes phlACB are necessary for the conversion of MAPG to 2,4-DAPG.
(E)-4-(3,5-dimethoxystyryl)phenyl acetate reduces glioblastoma cell colony formation and induces cell cycle arrest at the G2/M phase transition.
Rubrofusarin is an orange-red polyketid-type pigment formed in cultured fungi in response to L-tenuazonic acid at concentrations exceeding 0.5 mg per agar well.
Reticulol inhibits metastasis of B16F10 melanoma cells in mice in a dose-dependent manner, with intravenous administration resulting in greater metastasis suppression than peritonitis administration.
Artocarpetin is a potent tyrosinase inhibitor that contains a 4-resorcinol moiety in the B ring of its flavone skeleton.

Intravenous reticulol treatment in mice prolongs survival by approximately 50% and 90% at doses of 10 and 50 mg/kg, respectively, compared to untreated controls after 45 days of B16F10 melanoma implantation.
Artocarpetin is a furanoflavone isolated from Artocarpus heterophyllus wood that exhibits strong mushroom tyrosinase inhibitory activity with an IC50 lower than 50 µM.
2,2'-dihydroxy-4',6'-dimethoxychalcone exhibits cytotoxic activity against the human cancer cell line SGC-7901 with an IC50 value of 1.6 µg/ml.
Rubrofusarin is an aglycone that forms the core structure of the disaccharide compound rubrofusarin-6-13-Dgentiobioside, with a disaccharide moiety attached at the 6-position.
4-Bromophenyl-5-chloropyrazine-2-carboxamide exhibits antimycobacterial activity with a minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) of 12.5 μg/mL.
5-(methylthio)-1-beta-D-ribofuranosylimidazole-4-carboxamide exhibits significant antiviral activity against vaccinia virus in vitro.
4-(β-D-Ribofuranosyl)-2-pyridinecarboxamide is tested for antiviral activity against Influenza A/WSN/33 (H1N1) in a neuraminidase activity assay using MDCK cells and a fluorescent substrate.
A methoxy group at the ortho position of Ring C in 3-phenylisoquinolone suppresses replication of the Lee influenza viral strain but remains cytotoxic.

3-phenylisoquinolone derivatives with a hydroxyl or hydroxymethyl group at the ortho position of Ring C inhibit replication of PR8, HK, and Lee influenza viral strains with less cytotoxicity than an unsubstituted analogue.

A derivative containing residues of salts of 2-amino-3-cyclopropylpropanoicacid (compound 2) exhibits high inhibitory activity against influenza virus with an IC50 of 4.73 µg/mL, a selectivity index (SI) of 64, and minimal toxicity (CC50 > 300 µg/mL).
Substitution with a methoxy group at the 2-position of the benzoyl nucleus in 2-benzoylamino-3,5-dichloropyridine derivatives enhances HSV-1(MP) growth inhibition activity.

Spiromastilactone D displays potent antiviral activity against a panel of influenza A and B viruses and inhibits the interaction between the influenza virus hemagglutinin protein (HA) and sialic acid receptor, which is essential for viral attachment and entry.

4-(β-D-Ribofuranosyl)-2-pyridinecarboxamide inhibits the recombinant influenza A polymerase complex, as measured by its IC50 in a 40-minute 37°C reaction assay using a standard RNA synthesis buffer and polymerase complex.
Imidazole-4-carboxamide is used as a universal nucleobase in the synthesis of enantiomeric isodideoxynucleosides designed as biological mimics of anti-HIV active D- and L-isodideo-xyadenosines.
Quinoxalin-2-mercapto-acetyl-urea analogs exhibit low nanomolar potency in inhibiting viral egress in Marburg and Ebola VP40 VLP budding assays in HEK293T cells.

4-(β-D-Ribofuranosyl)-2-pyridinecarboxamide inhibits RNA replication catalyzed by recombinant influenza A polymerase complex in a nucleotide incorporation assay, with IC50 determined by measuring the concentration at which RNA product formation is reduced by 50%.
4-anilino-6-aminoquinazoline derivatives exhibit anti-MERS-CoV activity in Vero cells.
The 3-fluoro-1H-pyrrole-2-carboxamide moiety is found in compounds active against hepatitis B virus and is present in promising preclinical candidates.
Azo1,2,4-triazine derivatives have been described as inhibitors of influenza A and B virus replication, though the mechanism of action is uncharacterized.
3-amino-N-(4-bromophenyl)-4-methoxybenzamide is a representative N-aryl benzamide compound that exerts moderate anti-EV-A71 activity against multiple virus strains.
Hortensin exhibits strong antiviral potential against key proteins of SARS-CoV-2 involved in viral transcription, replication, and proliferation.

The presence of a 2-methoxy-4-sulfamoylbenzamide moiety in spirothiazolidinone compounds influences their anti-influenza virus activity, with the electron-withdrawing sulfonamide group on the para position of the benzene ring affecting activity against different influenza virus subtypes.
6-fluoro substitution on the indole ring of indole-2-carboxamides retains their antimycobacterial potency.

Quinoxaline-2-mercapto-acetylurea analogues inhibit viral egress of Marburg and Ebola viruses by targeting the PPxY motif in the viral matrix protein, thereby blocking virus-host interactions required for virus egress and dissemination.
Genkwanol A shows moderate activity against microtubule assembly in vitro with an IC50 value of 131 ± 3 mM.
Genkwanol A is moderately active against HIV-1 in vitro.
Genkwanol A shows moderate activity against microtubule polymerization with an IC50 value of 184 ± 6 µM in vitro.
Genkwanol A induces morphological deformation of Pyricularia oryzae mycelia with an MMDC value of 45.8 ± 0.5 µM.
Daphnodorin C does not affect chymase-dependent Ang II formation.
Daphnodorin C inhibits platelet 12-lipoxygenase and cyclooxygenase.
Daphnodorin C, like daphnodorin A and B, shares a similar structure but has a distinct effect on Ang II formation.
Daphnodorin C contains a solid carbonyl group at C-4' in its structure.
Neochamaejasmine A increases the bioavailability of Chamaechromone in rats by inhibiting breast cancer resistance protein (BCRP) and multidrug resistance protein 2 (MRP2)-mediated efflux.
NSD 2023 is a more effective inhibitor of mouse brain MAO in vivo than in vitro.
Biflavonols are significant in-vitro inhibitors of CYP enzymes in human liver microsomes.
UGT1A1 shows a substrate inhibition pattern for 3,7,4'-trihydroxyflavonol glucuronidation with Ks values in the range of 0.60–5.41 µM for both 3-O- and 7-O-glucuronidation.
M344 modulates several AD-related pathways and attenuates cognitive decline in a chronically treated AD mouse model.
5-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-γ-valerolactone-sulfate is detected in brain tissues of rats and pigs after treatment, indicating its ability to cross the blood-brain barrier.
R-(+)-3-(3-hydroxyphenyl)-N-propylpiperidine hydrochloride, a sigma-acting compound, does not alter methamphetamine-induced neurotoxicity in mice.
Q3GA is a principal human circulating metabolite derived from the in vivo phase II metabolism of quercetin.
R-(+)-3-(3-hydroxyphenyl)-N-propylpiperidine hydrochloride does not significantly alter NMDA-induced calcium flux in rat cortical neurons.
The rate of butamoxane hydroxylation in isolated rat hepatocytes is slower than in hepatic microsomes but identical to that in perfused liver.
In APP SW /PS1 dE9 transgenic mice, 2-[(pyridine-2-ylmethyl)-amino]-phenol ameliorates Aβ pathology and rescues memory deficits.
The Km values for 3,7,4'-trihydroxyflavonol glucuronidation by UGT1A1 are in the range of 0.13–1.57 µM.

4-Benzoyl-2,5-dimethyl-3,4-bis(hydroxymethyl)pyrrole is a pyrrole derivative used as a core structure for synthesizing bis(N-methylcarbamoyl) derivatives with potential antineoplastic activity and modified reactivity due to electronic perturbations from phenyl ring position.
The presence of glucose or amino acids does not repress m-toluate utilization in bacteria harboring the pWW0 plasmid.
3-keto-3-phenylpropanoic acid, when supplied as an ethyl ester at millimolar amounts, does not exhibit precursor activity in fungal metabolism.
N-methyl-phenylcarbamate can partially inhibit the regrowth of colistin-resistant subpopulations in Pseudomonas aeruginosa and Stenotrophomonas maltophilia.
Glycerol benzoate acts in a temperature- and concentration-dependent manner to eliminate male-to-male variability in sperm motility during cryopreservation.

Enantioenriched 1,2-diphenylbutan-1-one can be recovered in essentially quantitative yield (>95%) with less than 10% erosion in enantiomeric excess (<10%) after nickel-catalyzed borylation, due to the reduced basicity of the reaction medium.
HSD can scavenge and inactivate harmful radical species such as alkyl peroxyl radicals (E = 1.05 V) and superoxide radicals (E = 0.94 V) in the organism.
Phenylbutyrophenone derivatives function as antagonists at the 5-HT 2A, 5-HT 2C, and DA D 2 receptors, with selectivity profiles influenced by substituents at the phenyl ring.
Introduction of a 2-amino group onto the phenylbutyrophenone scaffold enhances binding affinity at all three target receptors (5-HT 2A, 5-HT 2C, and DA D 2) compared to the parent compound.

Monofluoroacetophenone is reduced to an alcohol with enantioselectivity that is influenced by the number of fluorine substituents at the ␣-position in dried cell-catalyzed reactions using NADP+ and cyclopentanol.

The antimicrobial activity of 2-methylbenzimidazoyl cinnamaldehyde analogs is enhanced in the presence of the efflux pump inhibitor PAbetaN, and they show synergistic effects with colistin against XDR Acinetobacter baumannii.
2-(3-methylbut-2-enyloxy)acetophenone (L1) reduces nitric oxide production in LPS-stimulated RAW 264.7 cells without affecting cell viability.
The first site of oxidation for HSD is the 3-phenoxyl group via a one-electron reduction.

(Z)-3-(phenyl)-1-(1,5-dimethyl-1H-pyrazole-3-yl)-3-hydroxyprop-2-en-1-one contains an antifungal pharmacophore site at N1---O4, which is linked to its biological activity against Fusarium oxysporum f. sp. albedinis.

2,2-DH-4-MBP is a benzophenone derivative sunscreen molecule that is photostable under UV treatment and, when co-entraped with enzyme in alumina matrices, provides the highest level of UV-C and UV-B protection among tested derivatives, with 2,2-DH-4-MBP being a five-fold and a four-fold increase in stability, and 31.5-fold and 8-fold increase in stability compared to free enzyme at UV-C and UV-B, respectively.

Bulky halide substituents at the 4-position of the phenylbutyrophenone moiety greatly improve affinity for the DA D 2 receptor and reduce 5-HT 2A affinity by approximately 35-fold, resulting in a reversed selectivity profile similar to classical neuroleptics.

HSD's high chemical reactivity can prevent free radical-induced injuries by directly interacting with radical oxygen species (ROS), inducing antioxidant enzyme activation, chelating metal ions, reducing α-tocopherol radicals, inhibiting oxidases, and reducing nitric oxide oxidative stress, and increasing the antioxidant effects of low molecular antioxidants.

2-phenylbutyrophenone is a chiral phenyl alcohol that does not achieve baseline separation by capillary electrochromatography with negatively charged sulfated β-CD as chiral selector under standard conditions.
2-fluoroacetophenone is converted to its corresponding phenyl acetate via HAPMO-catalyzed biotransformation by whole cells of P. fluorescens ACB.
m-toluate is a water-soluble, non-volatile aromatic compound that serves as a model substrate for studying biodegradation of aromatic hydrocarbons in the mycorrhizosphere.
Substituents on the phenyl group of furoyl pyrroloquinolones can be modified to improve oral bioavailability.
Furoyl pyrroloquinolones exhibit improved selectivity for PDE1 and PDE6 over PDE5 compared to sildenafil.
Furoyl pyrroloquinolones are synthesized by direct acylation of pyrroloquinolones with acid chlorides, or more efficiently by coupling with aryl- and heteroarylboronic acids using coupling acids.
Furoyl pyrroloquinolone is a highly selective and potent inhibitor of the phosphodiesterase 5 (PDE5) enzyme.
Ferroptocide exhibits immunostimulatory activity in a murine cancer model.
ACA exhibits anticancer effects in vivo in multiple myeloma models without causing detectable organ toxicity.
N-Cbz-L-phenylalanine cyanomethyl ester profoundly inhibits ascitic cell and fluid production in the Ehrlich ascites carcinoma mouse model.
Dihydronaphthalene glycinamide significantly reduces light emission in a bioluminescence imaging (BLI) study in SCID mice bearing luciferase-expressing MDA-MB-231-luc human breast cancer tumors.
Administration of metal complexes of o-Hydroxydithiobenzoate reverses tumor growth and induces apoptosis in tumor-bearing mice.
Nexturastat A is a selective HDAC6 inhibitor that, in combination with PD-1 immune blockade, significantly improves anti-tumor immune responses and reduces tumor growth in melanoma models.
Intraperitoneal administration of salirasib inhibits tumor growth in A549 and HTB58 cell nude mouse models.
The hydrochloride salt of dihydronaphthalene glycinamide was evaluated for its in vivo ability to disrupt tumor blood flow using dynamic bioluminescence imaging studies.
The metal complexes of o-Hydroxydithiobenzoate inhibit tumor growth in vitro and prolong survival in tumor-bearing mice.
N-[4-(5-bromo-2-pyrimidinyloxy)-3-chlorophenyl]-N'-(2- nitrobenzoyl) urea exhibits collateral sensitivity in vivo in CDDP- and MMC-resistant mouse leukemia sublines.
Gemcitabine is a novel antimetabolite with broad activity in preclinical solid tumor models.
2-bromo-3-(furan-2-yl)-3-oxopropionamide is a novel antibacterial compound discovered through virtual screening and 2.5D-QSAR modeling.
NK86-0279I induces prolongation of survival time in mice transplanted with Colon 26 carcinoma.
Di-2-pyridyl ketone 4,4-dimethylthiosemicarbazone has demonstrated significant antitumor activity in mouse models.
2-(2-furoyl)-4(5)-(2-furanyl)-1H-imidazole is an advanced glycation end product (AGE) that binds to a macrophage receptor and is internalized by macrophages.

N-(5-fluorobenzothiaol-2-yl)-2-guanidinothiazole-4-carboxamide exhibits antitumor activity in the micro metastatic 3LL Lewis lung carcinoma mouse model, demonstrating an excellent therapeutic index compared to the anticancer drug adriamycin.

Dietary intake of 3-Fluorobenzanthrone (FLA) affects hepatic aryl hydrocarbon hydroxylase (AHH) activity in rats, with lower AHH activities observed at dietary doses of 750 and 1500 mg/kg for 60 and 90 days, respectively.

Trifluoromethyl indanone can serve as a substrate in Zn-ProPhenol-catalyzed Mannich reactions with aromatic imines to afford beta-amino ketones with 71–98% yields and diastereoselectivity (dr > 20:1).
Nornicotine can be synthesized in optically pure enantiomers using an efficient and versatile enantioselective synthetic route.
Benzo[c]phenanthridinones are pharmaceutically interesting molecules for which new synthetic methods have been developed.
A linked binaphthyl dicarboxylate catalyst catalyzes 6-endo-fluorocyclization and deprotonative fluorination of allylic amides in a highly enantioselective manner.

2-Fluorophenylethanolamine exhibits fluorine-induced adrenergic selectivity, which is attributed to interactions between its benzylic OH group and an aromatic fluorine substituent, rather than conformational biases from intramolecular interactions.

3-fluorochromanone derivatives with electron-withdrawing substituents such as fluoro, bromo, and nitro groups on the aryl ring are efficiently reduced by asymmetric transfer hydrogenation to give cis-fluorohydrins with high diastereoselectivity and enantioselectivity.
Iso-propylphenyl sulfone reacts smoothly with imines in the (phenylsulfonyl)alkylation reaction, providing the expected products in good yield with excellent diastereoselectivity.
3-fluorochromanone serves as a substrate for ruthenium-catalyzed asymmetric transfer hydrogenation to yield enantioenriched cis-3-fluoro-chroman-4-ol derivatives.
1-chloro-2-acetoxy-3-(1-naphthyloxy)-propane is a substrate used for asymmetric hydrolysis by lipase from Pseudomonas cepacia and serves as a precursor for (S)-(-)-beta-blocker synthesis.

In the 5-(3-substituted amino-2-hydroxypropoxy)-3,4-dihydro-1(2H)-naphthalenone series, the potency of amino substituents for β-adrenergic blocking activity is i-Pr > sec-Bu > t-Bu > cyclopentyl > i-Bu.
Derivatives of 2-(3-isopropylamino-2-hydroxypropoxy) phenoxymethyl isoxazole possess both beta-adrenoblocking and alpha-adrenoblocking activity.
(S)-4-chlorophenylglycine can be synthesized in enantiomerically pure form via a two-step chemoenzymatic process involving palladium-catalyzed amidocarbonylation and enzymatic hydrolysis.

In phase 2a studies, vadadustat did not alter VEGF levels over the planned dose range for phase 3 clinical trials.

6-O-phenylascorbic acid permeates cell membranes slowly, resulting in its spin label reduction occurring only on the outer leaflet during membrane experiments.

2,5-bis(6-bromohexyl)-3,6-bis(5-(4-nitrophenyl)thiophen-2-yl)pyrrolo [3,4-c]pyrrole-1,4(2H,5H)dione nanoparticles (BNTP NPs) demonstrate excellent biocompatibility, low dark cytotoxicity, and high photothermal therapeutic efficiency in both in vitro and in vivo experiments.

5,6-bis(4-methoxyphenyl)-2H-pyridazin-3-one analogues have been synthesized and their structure-activity relationship evaluated for activity in collagen-induced arthritis models.

The binding of [125I]iodoglibenclamide to recombinant SUR1 is not systematically affected by nucleotides, as demonstrated by unpublished data.

1-[4-(5-chloro-2-methoxybenzamidoethyl)benzene sulfonamido]-1-cyclohexylamino-2-nitroethylene increases intracellular Ca2+ concentration in single pancreatic islet cells, an effect counteracted by verapamil, a Ca2+ entry blocker.

Metacridamide A is a compound with molecular formula C37H55NO6 and a molecular ion [M+Na]+ at m/z 632.3925 (calculated 632.3927), detected by HPLC with retention time 6.50 min.
