The 13C NMR spectrum of janthinocin C shows two signals at δ 166 ppm, consistent with the presence of both zδAbu and a second δa, δβ-unsaturated amide.

Janthinocin C contains a unique tenth residue with a molecular weight of 184 ± 18 (H2O) daltons and a molecular formula of C11H10N2O2, identified as dehydrotryptophan based on mass spectrometry and UV spectral data.
7-O-acetyl-19,20-epoxycytochalasin D shows strong cytotoxicity against HL-60 cell lines with an IC50 value of 25.83 µM.
Beauvericin A is an antimycobacterial and antiplasmodial cyclodepsipeptide isolated from Paecilomyces sp.

7-O-acetyl-19,20-epoxycytochalasin D is a 19,20-epoxycytochalasan isolated from Xylaria cf. curta, an endophyte associated with Solanum tuberosum, and shows cytotoxicity against HL-60 and MCF-7 cancer cell lines with IC50s in the range of 13.31–37.16 µM, while remaining inactive against A-549, SMMC-7721, and SW480 cell lines.
The dipeptide Phe-Val directly stimulates the transactivation of the Pept-1 gene promoter in Caco-2 cells.
Beauvericin A was identified in the CC-2-1 fraction of a compound mixture.
Lyngbyastatin 4 is a serine protease inhibitor with a selectivity profile distinct from that of other Ahp-containing cyclodepsipeptides.
lyngbyastatin 5 is a potent serine protease inhibitor that can cause false-positive inhibition of porcine pancreatic elastase activity in vitro.
Lyngbyastatin 5 is a secondary metabolite isolated from Lyngbya cf. confervoides.
Sansalvamide A amide is a natural product whose structure is related to unguisin A and other cyclic peptides containing nonproteinogenic amino acids.

In l-Phe-l-Val peptide structures, the phenyl group of l-Phe is involved in intermolecular interactions, including aromatic stacking and hydrogen bonding, to a higher degree than typically observed in peptide structures.
(E)-3-(2,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-1-(thiophen-2-yl)prop-2-en-1-one inhibits melanin content and intracellular tyrosinase in a dose-dependent manner in alpha-MSH and IBMX-induced B16F10 melanoma cells.
(E)-3-(2,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-1-(thiophen-2-yl)prop-2-en-1-one is a potential therapeutic agent for diseases associated with hyperpigment disorders due to its strong tyrosinase inhibitory activity.

(E)-3-(2,4-dihydroxyphenyl)-1-(thiophen-2-yl)prop-2-en-1-one is a potent competitive inhibitor of mushroom tyrosinase, exhibiting IC50 values of 0.013 µM for tyrosine hydroxylase and 0.93 µM for dopa oxidase.
KR-33028 significantly limits infarct size in the in vivo rat myocardial infarction model.
1-(2-Thienyl)-1-heptanone is a targeted metabolite implicated in the metabolic reprogramming induced by semaglutide in rats post-myocardial infarction.
4-hydroxy-2-chlorocinnamic acid is a novel compound produced by cinnamate 4-hydroxylase (C4H) from a substrate analogue with a 2-chloro aromatic side chain.
1-(2-Thienyl)-1-heptanone is upregulated by myocardial infarction and is reversed by semaglutide treatment in rats.

KR-33028 is a bicyclic acylguanidine compound under evaluation as a cardioprotective agent for ischemia and reperfusion injury, with an oral LD50 greater than 1500 mg/kg in rats and a relatively low toxicity profile.
2-chloro-2′,5′-dihydroxychalcone exhibits more potent cytotoxic activity against cancer cells and greater anti-angiogenic activity in vitro than 2-chloro-2,5-dihydroxychalcone.
1-(2-Thienyl)-1-heptanone is downregulated in the myocardium of semaglutide-treated rats after myocardial infarction, implicating it in the metabolic effects of semaglutide.
1-(2-Thienyl)-1-heptanone is significant in metabolic regulation following myocardial infarction.
4-hydroxychalcone is the most potent inhibitor of 17␤-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase among the compounds studied.

KR-33028 is a cardioprotective agent under preclinical evaluation for ischemia and reperfusion injury, shown to recover cardiac contractability above 60% of basal rate pressure product, attenuate cardiac contracture, and protect myocyte damage in the isolated rat heart ischemia model.
2,4-dihydroxy-3'-methoxy-4'-ethoxychalcone is a flavonoid constituent that notably inhibits proliferation of the multiple myeloma (MM) cell line U266.
2,6'-Dihydroxy-4'-methoxychalcone (DMC) exhibits significant activity against promastigotes and intracellular amastigotes of Leishmania amazonensis.
4-hydroxychalcone exhibits anti-aromatase activity, and its inhibitory effect is reduced when hydroxylated at position 2Ј, resulting in 2Ј,4-dihydroxychalcone.
The O-dealkylation metabolite 2,4-dihydroxybenzophenone (2,4OH-BP) of 2-methoxy-4-hydroxybenzophenone in rat blood decreases much more slowly over time than the parent compound.

2-chloro-2',5'-dihydroxychalcone is a 2,5-dihydroxychalcone derivative with an electron-withdrawing substituent on the B ring, making it the most potent anti-angiogenic compound among tested 2,5-dihydroxychalcones.
4-hydroxychalcone is a potent inhibitor of 17beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase activity in human placental microsomes, with an IC50 value of 16 µM.
4-hydroxychalcone, a chalconoid with one OH group eliminated, exhibits a loss in activity of approximately 20-fold compared to chalconoids with both OH groups present.

dimethyl-5-methyl-1-(2-nitrobenzoyl)pyrrolo[1,2-a]quinoline-2,3-dicarboxylate is a substituted pyrrolo[1,2-a]quinoline derivative with physicochemical properties including FT-IR, 1H-NMR, and 13C-NMR data.
N-[4-(5-bromo-2-pyrimidinyloxy)-3-chlorophenyl]-N'-(2- nitrobenzoyl) urea exhibits collateral sensitivity in vivo in CDDP- and MMC-resistant mouse leukemia sublines.
Nitrobenzamide derivatives function as photonucleases that can be conjugated to acridine derivatives to enhance DNA interaction and activity.

Nitrobenzamide CB1954 is a cytotoxic compound that interacts with DT-diaphorase, with its aziridinyl group pointing toward Tyr155 and its amide group near a hydrophobic pocket next to Tyr128 in the active site.
Zoligratinib resistance can be promoted by certain FGFR2 mutations.

The copper(II) and platinum(II) complexes of 5-(2-hydroxybenzoyl)-3-methyl-1-(2-pyridinyl)pyrazol-4-carboxylic acid methyl ester are more efficient in inducing leukaemia cell death than the palladium(II) complex.
MitoBlocker-60 potently inhibits proliferation of human K562 leukemia cells in a dose-dependent manner without inducing cell death.
The reduction and covalent protein binding of 4-iodo-3-nitrobenzamide in E-ras 20 cells is associated with apparent nitro-reductase activity, which is not present in CV-1 non-tumorigenic cells.

5-(2-hydroxybenzoyl)-3-methyl-1-(2-pyridinyl)pyrazol-4-carboxylic acid methyl ester forms Ni(II) complexes that exhibit relatively high cytotoxic activity towards HL-60 and NALM-6 leukaemia cells and WM-115 melanoma cells.
5-(2-hydroxybenzoyl)-3-methyl-1-(2-pyridinyl)pyrazol-4-carboxylic acid methyl ester forms complexes with platinum(II), palladium(II), and copper(II) that exhibit lower cytotoxicity than cisplatin.
3,4,5-trimethoxybenzophenone arrangement confers the highest cytotoxicity activity among tested analogues in MDA-MB-231 triple-negative breast cancer cells.

N-[4-(5-bromo-2-pyrimidinyloxy)-3-chlorophenyl]-N'-(2-nitrobenzoyl) urea (HO-221) is a novel antitumor compound with significant activity against various murine tumors and human xenografts, especially effective against solid tumors, and shows no cross-resistance to ten clinically used antitumor agents in humans.
Chrysosporazine B is noncytotoxic and reverses doxorubicin drug resistance in P-glycoprotein overexpressing colon carcinoma cells (SW620 Ad300).
A 5-azido-2-nitrobenzoate analogue of paclitaxel has been synthesized and incorporated into studies of the paclitaxel binding site.
(Z)-3,4,5,4-tetramethoxystilbene inhibits EGFR phosphorylation and activation in gefitinib-resistant lung cancer cells.

Ethyl 4-oxo-2-phenyl-1,4-dihydroquinoline-6-carboxylate (AS1712) can overcome P-glycoprotein efflux pump and beta-tubulin alterations that confer resistance to microtubule-targeting drugs in cancer cells.

N-[4-(5-bromo-2-pyrimidinyloxy)-3-chlorophenyl]-N'-(2- nitrobenzoyl) urea shows antitumor effects in mice bearing leukemia cell lines resistant to multiple other antitumor agents, including daunomycin, mitomycin C, cisplatin, 5-fluorouracil, cytosine arabinoside, methotrexate, cyclophosphamide, carboquone, adriamycin, and vincristine.
4,9-dinitrophenanthrene is used as a chemical reagent to treat breast cancer cells (MCF-7, SKBR3, and MDA-MB-231) for 48 hours at concentrations ranging from 10 nM to 1 μM.
Epoxymollugin (compound 3) exhibits stronger DNA topoisomerase I and II inhibitory activity and greater cytotoxicity in the MCF-7 cell line compared to mollugin, due to epoxidation.
Nitrobenzamide derivatives, such as CB 1954, are substrates for DT diaphorase, and all tested co-factors for menadione reduction are equally effective co-factors for their reduction.
EB-PSMA-617 enhances cellular uptake, internalization, and retention of radioactivity, resulting in improved therapeutic effect compared to other PSMA-617 variants.
EB-PSMA-617 is an albumin binding radioligand that shows higher tumor uptake than PSMA-617 and longer blood circulation (12.2 ± 0.8 %ID/g at 1 hour post-injection) in PET studies using Y-86.
EB-PSMA-617 exhibits higher tumor uptake at 4 h (40.9 ± 6.7 %ID/g) and retained uptake over 24–48 h (74.5 ± 11.0 %ID/g at 24 h, 77.3 ± 6.2 %ID/g at 48 h) in PSMA-expressing tumors.
EB-PSMA-617 is an albumin-binding radioligand that specifically targets PSMA, as evidenced by its kidney uptake being blocked by 64% co-injection with excess unlabeled EB-PSMA-617.
EB-PSMA-617 shows slower blood clearance (t1/2 5.6 h) compared to 86 Y-IP-PSMA-617 in PET studies.
PSMA I&T is a PSMA inhibitor that has been described as a theranostic agent for PET imaging and is under investigation for its biodistribution and radiation dosimetry.
PSMA I&T is a radiopharmaceutical that targets PSMA and has demonstrated comparable PSMA affinity and in vivo uptake and kinetics to 68 Ga-PSMA HBED-CC in LNCaP-tumor xenograft models.
177 Lu-PSMA I&T is a radiolabeled compound based on the PSMA-binding inhibitor PSMA I&T, developed for imaging and therapy targeting prostate cancer.

PSMA I&T is a second-generation theranostics tracer developed to enhance PSMA affinity and pharmacokinetic properties by incorporating a peptidic linker unit, which increases lipophilic interaction with the PSMA enzyme.
DOTAGA-ffk(Sub-KuE) is a second-generation DOTAGA-functionalized PSMA ligand used for comparison of PSMA binding affinity in studies of PSMA I&T constructs.
Azovan blue is used to estimate plasma volume in clinical investigations.
Azovan blue is an anionic trypanocide with trypanocidal activity against T. rhodesiense.
Azovan blue is a reagent used to estimate plasma volume in blood samples.
Azovan blue is used as a component (0.5%) in a solution for experimental setup involving mesenteric tissue and Locke's solution.
Azovan blue is a bis-2,4-disulphonate-substituted dye with a central dimethylbenzidine moiety, differing from trypan blue only in the position of its sulphonic acid groups.
Thrombin induces essentially irreversible homologous desensitization of platelet TxA2 synthesis.
Application of TRag at 1 µM increases intracellular Ca 2+ concentration in most rat astrocytes (73/76 cells).
Pep2-EVKI is a PICK1 selective inhibitor that does not affect AMPA receptor function on its own.
pep2-EVKI inhibits kainate receptor-mediated synaptic transmission by preventing PICK1 interaction with kainate receptors.
Pep2-EVKI is a cell-permeable peptide that disrupts GluR2 trafficking and attenuates cocaine-induced reinstatement of drug seeking when administered into the nucleus accumbens core or shell.
A 1-mM bolus of 2-Aminodihydro-6-methyl-4H-1,3-thiazine significantly reduces plasma nitric oxide levels in suspended animals to well below pre-suspension or post-suspension saline-treated levels.

Infusion of 2-Amino-dihydro-6-methyl-4H-1,3-thiazine enhances the baroreflex response, as evidenced by an increased shift of the baroreflex curve to the right and increases in both the MAP 50 value and the gain.

2-Amino-dihydro-6-methyl-4H-1,3-thiazine bolus treatment modifies baroreflex sensitivity for heart rate in suspended rats by decreasing the curvature coefficient of the mean arterial pressure response, indicating expanded MAP response range, and increasing baroreceptor input by expanding the heart rate range.
AMT (2-aminodihydro-6-methyl-4H-1,3-thiazine) is a specific inhibitor of the inducible nitric oxide synthase (iNOS=NOS II) isoform.

AMT (2-amino-dihydro-6-methyl-4H-1,3-thiazine) is a nitric oxide synthase II (NOS II) inhibitor that reduces plasma nitric oxide levels and attenuates the post-suspension reduction in mean arterial pressure in rats.

The presence of a 5-methyl oxothiazolidine nucleus with a bibenzyl nucleus enhances the fungicidal activity of the compound against Fusarium oxysporium and Penicillium citrinum, causing complete inhibition of mycelial growth.
4H-pyridopyrimidine analogs exhibit broad-spectrum inhibition of bacterial growth.
Aminothiochromane-based carboxamide ligands are designed to promote hydrogen bonding and van der Waals interactions in the active site of HIV-1 protease.
7h demonstrates increased antibacterial activity against Pseudomonas aeruginosa compared to other tested cephalosporins.
Aminothiochromane is a biologically active compound that can be obtained by reduction of nitro-substituted thiochromanes without loss of enantioselectivity.
5-amino-8-methylquinolone derivatives exhibit potent antibacterial activity against Gram-positive bacteria and show reduced chromosomal toxicity compared to 8-methylquinolone derivatives.

D-1,4,6-trideoxy-4,4-difluoronojirimycin is a gem-difluoromethylenated azasugar whose biological activity as a fucosyltransferase inhibitor can be influenced by the presence or absence of the C4-OH group, with the C2-OH and C3-OH groups being important for good inhibitor binding.
The core hexahydrobenzo[4,5]thieno[2,3-d]pyrimidine structure exhibits inhibition of Plasmodium dihydrofolate reductase (PfDHFR).
Thiazolidinylpyrimidine compounds inhibit mycelial dry matter and sporulation, as well as RNA and DNA synthesis, in fungi.
Amino-thiochroman derivatives are used as P2 ligands in the design of HIV-1 protease inhibitors to promote effective hydrogen bonding interactions with backbone atoms in the S2 subsite.

3-acetoxymethylcephem (compound 1d) exhibits relatively broad antibacterial activity against both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria, with good antibacterial activity retained against a wide range of organisms.
The N-alkyl-1,2-dihydro-2-thioxo-3-pyridinecarbothioamide structure is the most active among tested analogues in preliminary microbiological assays.
7h is a methoxyimino cephem cephalosporin that exhibits well-balanced antibacterial activity against a wide range of Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria.
Adamantyl-triazole (compound 5b) was produced as a potential lead antibiotic for use against MRSA and VRE strains using click chemistry methodology.
Turmerol, present in C. longa, contributes to the synergistic antibacterial efficacy of the polyherbal Unani tooth powder formulation by acting on the bacterial cell wall and inhibiting enzymes.
A buffer containing 0.38% (w/v) polyoxyethylene bis(acetic acid) at pH 8.6 has low conductivity due to the high molecular weight of the compound.

1,4-diazabicyclo[2.2.2]octanium diacetate acts as an acidic bis ionic liquid catalyst for the ultrasound-assisted synthesis of benzodiazepines from OPD and aldehydes, providing 93–98% yield in 8–15 minutes at room temperature.

2,5-Dihydro-3H-pyrazolo [4,3-c]quinolin-3-one derivatives (PQs) are synthesized as ligands for the benzodiazepine receptor (BzR) and are studied for their structure-efficacy relationships and classification in drug design.
Polyoxyethylene bis(acetic acid) is used as a component of the buffer at 0.031% (w/v) concentration in low-conductivity electrophoresis for protein separation.
Phenylpiperazine-phenylacetate derivatives are designed as rapid recovery hypnotic agents with high affinity for the GABAA receptor and low affinity for other off-target receptors.
Polyoxyethylene bis(acetic acid) is a nontoxic polymer used as the backbone for the high-molecular-weight carbonic anhydrase inhibitor F-3500.
Triazino-isoquinoline derivatives have been synthesized and their binding interactions with central and peripheral benzodiazepine binding sites have been characterized.
Ethyl 8-ethynyl-5,6-dihydro-5-methyl-6-oxo-4H-imidazo[1,5-a][1,4]benzodiazepine exhibits some degree of selectivity for R5-containing GABA A receptor subtypes compared to its parent (C8-H) compound.
2-amino-5-(2-phenoxybenzyl)-1,3,4-oxadiazole compounds act as benzodiazepine analogs and exhibit superior binding affinity toward GABA A receptors compared with diazepam.
1,4-diazabicyclo[2.2.2]octanium diacetate is an acidic bis ionic liquid used as a catalyst in the multicomponent synthesis of benzodiazepines under ultrasound irradiation.
1,2,3,4,7-pentamethylbicyclo[2.2.1]hept-2-ene-5,6-dicarboximide derivatives have been synthesized as potential anxiolytic agents.
Methyl 1,5-diphenyl-2,3-diazabicyclo[3.1.0]hex-2-ene-6-carboxylate exhibits good binding affinity for GABA A R5 receptors.
Benzo[c]phenanthridinones are pharmaceutically interesting molecules for which new synthetic methods have been developed.
4,5-dihydrobenzo[g]indole-3-carboxamide analogues have been synthesized and their structure-activity relationships studied as potential antipsychotic agents.
1-methyl-1,4-benzodiazepine-2-one can be used to synthesize Rh-N-heterocyclic carbene (NHC) complexes that act as intermediates in intramolecular Rh-catalyzed C-2-alkylation of azoles.
Thiazolobenzodiazepine derivatives act as inhibitors of [3H]flunitrazepam binding to membranes from cerebellum or hippocampus in a concentration-dependent manner.
Imidazodiazepine compounds act as partial inverse agonists at central type benzodiazepine receptors.
Pyrazolopyrroloquinolin-3-one derivatives inhibit the binding of [3H] flunitrazepam at central benzodiazepine receptors (BZRs), with affinity profiles consistent across tested derivatives.

Ethyl pyrazolo[1,5-a]quinazoline 3-carboxylate, with a completely aromatized tricyclic system, is the only 5-deaza-scaffold compound among tested analogues that exhibits binding affinity to the GABAA receptor.
2-amino-5-(2-phenoxybenzyl)-1,3,4-oxadiazole is a potent benzodiazepine analogue examined for its binding orientation in the GABA A receptor binding site by molecular docking.
Altrenogest is an oral progestogen used to regulate the estrous cycle in mares, particularly in late spring or after photoperiod stimulation.
4,9-estradien-3-one derivatives with 4'-substituted phenyl groups at the 1µ-position constitute a new class of steroidal antihormones known as antigestagens.
Estra-4,9(10)-dien-3-one reacts with peracids to yield monoepoxides in high yield.
RU 486 is an antiprogestational compound that acts by competitively binding to the progesterone and cortisol receptor.

17 beta hydroxy-11 beta, 4-dimethylaminophenyl-17 alpha-propynyl estra 4,9 diene-3-one (RU 38486) acts as a glucocorticoid receptor antagonist and blocks the growth inhibitory effect of dexamethasone in NHIK 3025 cells.

17 beta-hydroxy-11 beta,4-dimethylaminophenyl-17 alpha-propynyl estra-4,9-diene-3-one, also known as RU 486, is an antiglucocorticoid that blocks cortisol-induced phosphorylation of the glucocorticoid receptor in intact WEHI-7 mouse thymoma cells.

Estra-4,9-dien-3-one is a glucocorticoid receptor antagonist (GRA) and progesterone receptor antagonist (PRA) that reduces weight gain and decreases elevated insulin levels and weight gain in olanzapine-treated rats.

Estra-4,9-dien-3-one (mifepristone) is a glucocorticoid receptor antagonist (GRA) and progesterone receptor antagonist (PRA) that reduces weight gain observed during olanzapine or risperidone treatment.
Mexrenoate potassium exhibits equivalent antihypertensive responses to spironolactone in dogs with established hypertension (Page model).
ADD forms both syn and anti isomers via non-sterically hindered keto groups during in vitro metabolism by isolated hepatocytes.
6-methylenandrost-4-ene-3,17-dione is not a time-dependent inactivator of aromatase.
Physalin C is a proteasome-targeted anticancer agent.
4-demethyl-6-deoxydoxorubicin is the most cytotoxic anthracycline analogue tested in P388 cells.
22-allyl-digitoxigenin exerts a reversible positive inotropic effect in sheep cardiac Purkinje fibres at a concentration of 1.1 x 10^-5 M.
Hydroxy derivatives of HMPD (20-hydroxymethylpregna-1,4-diene-3-one, XIV) can exhibit cytotoxicity towards HeLa cancer lines, a property not observed with the non-hydroxylated substrate.
Ferroptocide exhibits immunostimulatory activity in a murine cancer model.
17α-methyl-19-nortestosterone (normethandrone) is a potent inhibitor of glucagon-induced hyperglycemia.
Estrone-2,3-quinone (E2-2,3-Q) forms stable adducts with epidermal chromosomal DNA, as demonstrated by 32P-postlabeling and HPLC analysis.
Normethandrone does not produce a statistically different magnitude of BSP retention compared to fluoxymesterone, methyltestosterone, and norethindrone.
Androstenylaminobenzoic acid derivatives exhibit cytotoxic activity, with their potency influenced by the nature of the heterocyclic ring incorporated into the steroid moiety.
Trimethyloxosulfonium hydroxide serves as an effective methylating agent for the regioselective methylation of inosine to produce 7-methylinosine under aqueous conditions.
MeAIB is not an inhibitor of System N in hepatocytes.
A thiophene S-oxide intermediate is implicated as a possible species involved in the inactivation of CYP 2C19 and CYP 2C9 by ticlopidine and tienilic acid, respectively.
Hydroquinone-glutathione is used as a substrate to study hydroquinone metabolism in hepatocytes, including optimization of substrate concentration and incubation time.
Diastereoisomeric thiophene-S-oxide dimers have been detected in in vitro and in vivo studies, providing evidence for the formation of thiophene-S-oxide.
Phenobarbital pretreatment potentiates hepatic necrosis produced by thioacetamide S-oxide.
DPPD does not affect the induction of peroxisomal acyl CoA oxidase activity by nafenopin in rat hepatocytes.
1-monocarboxymethylthiodecane administration increases peroxisomal beta-oxidation and mitochondrial carnitine palmitoyltransferase activity in rat hepatocytes.
The transfer of [14C]acetaldehyde from acetaldehyde-protein complexes to K562 cells is reduced by treatment with thioctic acid or dihydrolipoic acid.
SDS-PAGE analysis shows that [14C]acetaldehyde binds to many platelet proteins at neutral pH.
The metabolism of α-nitroanisole has been studied in freshly isolated adult rat hepatocytes.
The rate of butamoxane hydroxylation in isolated rat hepatocytes is slower than in hepatic microsomes but identical to that in perfused liver.
DCVC can be metabolically activated to reactive species in intact cells and in LLC-PKI cell homogenates.

1-aminobenzyltriazole, a cytochrome P450 inhibitor, removes seven of nine radioactive protein spots modified by cytochrome P450-mediated reactive metabolites of diclofenac, indicating its specificity for these protein modifications.
The conversion of Hydroquinone-glutathione to a di-glutathione conjugate in hepatocytes requires rate constants that are optimized to account for mass balance in metabolic studies.
Inhibition of FALDH (fatty acid lipase D-hydrolase) increases the amounts of 2EHD GSH conjugates and protein adducts formed in HepG2 cell lysates.
(4-(methylthio)phenyl)diaziridine does not inhibit P450 2B6 in a mechanism-based manner.
1,3-dimethyluric acid is the sole metabolite produced from theophylline metabolism in isolated rat hepatocytes, with stable production regardless of cold preservation time or storage solution.
Pregnane-16alpha-carbonitrile functions as a CYP3A transcriptional activator, causing a dose-dependent increase in CYP3A transcription in primary human hepatocytes.
3,5-Mer-NAPQI is a cytotoxic metabolite that markedly decreases the fluorescence of a 17 kDa microsomal protein in rat liver microsomes.
5-hydroxyl-5-methyl-2-hexenoic acid is a promising candidate for new antifungal agents due to its strong in vitro and in vivo antifungal activity.
5-hydroxyl-5-methyl-2-hexenoic acid moderately inhibits hyphal growth of Rhizoctonia solani with an IC50 of 61.64 mg/L.

5-hydroxyl-5-methyl-2-hexenoic acid exhibits strong in vitro antifungal activity against Botrytis cinerea, Cladosporium cucumerinum, and Corynespora cassiicola with IC50 values of 32.45, 27.17, and 30.66 mg/L, respectively.

In greenhouse conditions, 5-hydroxyl-5-methyl-2-hexenoic acid controls diseases caused by Botrytis cinerea, Cladosporium cucumerinum, and Corynespora cassiicola with 71.42%, 78.63%, and 65.13% disease control at 350 mg/L, respectively.
5-hydroxyl-5-methyl-2-hexenoic acid is an antifungal compound isolated from the mycelium of Actinoplanes sp. HBDN08.
Fusion of insulin to a fluorenylmethyloxycarbonyl derivative of 16-sulfanyl hexadecenoic acid results in a 5-fold increase in plasma half-life of insulin after IV administration in rat models.
TTA is a metabolite that can be quantified in fish tissues (gills, heart, kidney, liver) using LC-MS/MS, with standard concentrations ranging from 0.11 to 5.50 µg/ml depending on the tissue.
CVA (compound X) is used to induce differentiation and modulate γ-globin gene expression in JK-1 cells at a concentration of 50 mM for 48 hours.
TTA decreases the incorporation of palmitic acid into triacylglycerol and reduces triacylglycerol synthesis from glycerol in hepatocytes.
EPA-EA has affinity for both cannabinoid (CB) and non-cannabinoid (non-CB) receptors.
EPA-EA is a non-oxidatively converted endogenous omega-3 eCB that acts as a precursor to more potent bioactive molecules through CB-independent and CB-dependent pathways.
CVAA molecules are displaced from polar active sites of the matrix by 1,2-ethanedithiol during extraction cycles.
Mevaldic acid can be converted to mevalonic acid in liver enzyme systems, as demonstrated in experiments where cholesterol synthesis is blocked by ribonuclease pretreatment.
EPA-EA possesses anti-proliferative and anti-inflammatory properties and may modulate the inflammatory cascade.
TTA is a dietary supplement used at concentrations of 0.3% (TTA-L) and 0.6% (TTA-H) in fish oil diets for analytical determination in fish tissue.
Mevaldic acid is a substrate for mevalonic acid dehydrogenase, and its catalytic activity is measured by Michaelis-Menten analysis at pH 6.2 with DPNH or TPNH as reducing agents.
Mevaldic acid may serve as a precursor of mevalonic acid rather than being derived from it, as supported by experimental evidence.
Eicosapentaenoic acid-ethanolamine is an omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acid-ethanolamine derivative identified as an excellent anti-inflammatory agent that targets Nur77.
Octadecenoic and octadecadienoic acid display differential expression in hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) patients undergoing local ablation versus palliative care.
(2S,3R)-Hydroxycitric acid inhibits pancreatic α-amylase and small intestine α-glucosidase.

N-(1H-indazol-5-yl)acetamide (compound 5) is a paracetamol analogue that shows no significant activity at CB1, CB2 cannabinoid receptors, or FAAH, but displays analgesic activity in the mouse formalin test comparable to paracetamol (200 mg/kg).
N-(1H-indazol-5-yl)acetamide (compound 5) behaves like an inhibitor of cyclooxygenases in whole blood assays.
N-(1H-indazole-5yl)acetamide is a ROCK inhibitor with improved potency, showing IC50 values of 65 µM for ROCK1 and 40 µM for ROCK2.
4'-Fluoroacetanilide is a poor inhibitor of recombinant caspase-3, a cysteine-dependent enzyme.
4'-Fluoroacetanilide is a potent inactivator of recombinant P-glycoprotein, a cysteine-dependent enzyme.
2-acetylamino-quinoacridine is a potent telomerase inhibitor with an IC50 of 0.375 µM.
The K M of 3-(Acetamido)-N,N,N-trimethylanilinium (ATMA) for acetylcholinesterase is 47 µM, as determined by kinetic analysis.
2-acetylamino-quinoacridine exhibits low cytotoxicity in the NCI in vitro assay, with a mean GI50 of 12 µM.
3-(Acetamido)-N,N,N-trimethylanilinium (ATMA) inhibits the hydrolysis of o-nitrophenyl acetate by acetylcholinesterase with a K I of 42 µM.

3-(Acetamido)-N,N,N-trimethylanilinium (ATMA) acts as a pure competitive inhibitor of acetylthiocholine hydrolysis by acetylcholinesterase, with a K I of 30 µM at low acetylthiocholine concentrations.
4-fluoroacetanilide is metabolized in rabbit and rat liver microsomes to a significant extent via para-hydroxylation, which involves metabolic de-fluorination and formation of 4-acetamidophenol.

6-bromo-N2-(1H-indazol-5-yl)pyridine-2,3-diamine is a chemical compound characterized by 1H NMR (300 MHz, DMSO-d6) with signals at δ 13.41 (s, 1H), 8.21 (s, 1H), 8.06 (d, J = 8.3 Hz, 1H), 7.90 (d, J = 1.4 Hz, 1H), 7.73 (d, J = 8.7 Hz, 1H), 7.46 (d, J = 8.3 Hz, 1H), 7.36 (dd, J = 8.7, 1.8 Hz, 1H), 3.64–3.53 (m, 1H), 2.44–2.38 (m, 2H), 2.13–2.01 (m, 2H), 1.93–1.78 (m, 2H), and shows a melting point of 252–253 °C.

2-(1H-indazol-6-yl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazol-5-yl benzamide derivatives act as potent FLT3 inhibitors, with the most potent compound (8r) showing nanomolar IC50 and high selectivity over 42 protein kinases.
2-acetylamino-quinoacridine shows no mechanistic similarity (PCC < 0.5) to the methylamine quinoacridinium salts 20c and 20d in COMPARE analysis, indicating a different biological mechanism.
4'-Fluoroacetanilide is an alkylating compound that covalently modifies thiol moieties in proteins via Michael-addition reactions.
3-(Acetamido)-N,N,N-trimethylanilinium (ATMA) is a substrate analogue that activates its own acetylation by forming ternary complexes with both A- and P-sites of acetylcholinesterase.
2-acetylamino-quinoacridine is a neutral 2-substituted quinoacridine derivative used as a seed compound in COMPARE analysis to assess mechanistic similarity with other compounds.

Intraperitoneal administration of 4-acetamidophenylboronic acid pinacol ester to rats with orchiectomy-induced cognitive deficits ameliorates disrupted behavior and neuronal damage caused by hormonal deprivation.
2-acetylaminoquinoacridine can be synthesized by acetylation of the exocyclic amino group of a quinoacridine amine followed by methylation at N-13.

N-(2-(1H-indazol-6-yl)-1H-benzo[d]imidazol-5-yl)benzamide derivatives can be structurally optimized as potent FLT3 inhibitors, with compound 8r showing strong inhibition of FLT3 and FLT3 mutants relevant to AML.
Removal of EDTA by dialysis leads to rapid accumulation of CEOOH in LDL, even when precautions are taken to minimize oxidation.

CEOOH isomers are formed in oxidized LDL and their distribution can be evaluated using the DPPP-TLC blot/GC-EI-MS method, which aids in understanding the mechanisms contributing to atherogenic oxidized LDL formation in vivo.
CEOOH isomers are involved in the initiation of lipid peroxidation, which is a key step leading to the formation of oxidized LDL and foam cells associated with atherosclerosis.

CEOOH (cholesterol oxidation oxidation product) accumulates in LDL during and after ultracentrifugation-based isolation methods, with levels as low as pmol/mg Ubiquinol-10 Vitamin E LDL protein after centrifugation.
Significant accumulation of CEOOH in LDL (up to nmol/mg LDL protein) is associated with a linear decrease in LDL vitamin E content, with up to a 70% loss in some samples.
CHMDP produces a large decrease in the calcium absorption capacity of the duodenum.
1,4-dioxaspiro[2.3]hexane is a versatile building block used in the synthesis of cell signaling and immunomodulatory molecules such as dihydrosphingosines, sphingosines, and phytosphingosines.
Glycine-C l4 is produced from threonine-1,2-Cl4 by rat liver particles, though less rapidly than by whole liver homogenates.
3-chloro-3-pyridinol is used as a reagent for esterification with carboxylic acids such as methyl-1H-indole-5-carboxylate to synthesize 3-chloropyridinyl ester-derived SARS-CoV-2 3CLpro inhibitors.
Tetrachloro-2,3,5,6-tetrahydroxybenzene (TPAA) efficiently regenerates LEG4+ dye by electron transfer from TPAA˙+.
The formula of α-L-sorbopyranose is structurally compared to that of L-iditol to illustrate the importance of functional group arrangement for phagostimulating activity in polyols.
Dichloropropylene oxide shows a pronounced decrease in mutagenicity upon the addition of rat liver $9 fractions, which is attributed to membrane-bound hydrolase activity.
TTC is used as a fluorescent marker incorporated into bones and teeth to assess vaccine bait uptake in animals, enabling identification of a line mark and estimation of bait consumption periods.

Dichloro (1,2-diaminocyclohexane) Platinum (II) can be encapsulated in PBA-PEG-PLGA micellar nanocarriers designed to specifically target sialic acid (SA) overexpressed on the surface of cancer cells.

Glycidol acetate can be resolved by interfacial esterases from marine invertebrates, achieving an enantiomeric ratio of 2.67 (S-selective) when using a biocatalyst derived from Stichodactyla helianthus.
Tetra-acetyl arabinosylcytosine is more efficiently transported across the intestinal barrier than N-acetyl arabinosylcytosine and tri-O-acetyl arabinosylcytosine.
TRN undergoes photoisomerization upon exposure to radiation at 420 nm, making it susceptible to photodegradation.
2,2-dimethyl-1,3-dioxan-4-one-substituted compound 6f, a potent acetylcholinesterase (AChE) inhibitor, has the highest AChE inhibitory activity in biological experiments.

IP-1,2-O has been shown to form adducts at the N-terminal valine of hemoglobin in the blood of mice and rats administered IP or a commercially available IP monoepoxide mixture (IPO commercial) containing about 95% IP-1,2-O and 5% IP-3,4-O.
X-irradiation of α-L-sorbose single crystals at room temperature produces at least 12 distinct radical species, as detected by ENDOR and EI-EPR spectroscopy.
